LAW AND MOTION 301, JUDGE BUSCH MADE A DISCLOSURE ABOUT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND THAT HE DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS BOARD OF SUPERVISORS AND CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 22, 2011 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 301 TO BE HEARD TOGETHER WITH ANOTHER MOTION SET ON THAT DATE IN THIS DEPARTMENT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT BOARD OF SUPERVISORS, CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FOURTH CASE IS BARRED AT LEAST BY RES JUDICATA AND THE STATUTE OF LIMITATION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189

PLAINTIFF ANDY NICHOLAW'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ERROR MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM THE JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT BRING TO COURT A PROPER PROOF OF SERVICE ATTESTING TO SERVICE THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 1005. PLAINTIFF WAS ADVISED TO READ CCP 391(B)(2) AND 1008 AND CRC 3.1112. AFTER THE HEARING, PLAINTIFF ANDY NICHOLAW'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF ERROR AND MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER, HAS NO PROOF OF SERVICE AND HAS NO MERIT. DEFENDANT IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL A. KAREN, CSR # 8189 (301)
